items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Matches the approved translation verbatim, preserving the mock-heroic phrasing (“vague doubts haunt me”) and the clipped parallel structure that makes the bureaucrat’s melodrama funny. Also correctly avoids turning it into simple possession (“Shpak has...”), keeping the intended implication that these are the items connected to each person’s loss/suspicion.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved version. It keeps the era-clash comedy: banal modern politeness formulas and repetitive self-introduction (“the Tsar”) instead of archaic court speech, and the drunken, socially awkward rhythm is preserved.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Perfect match. The imperative “I demand” preserves the sudden authoritarian, power-drunk persona shift that is the core of the joke.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Keeps the elevated, archaic melancholy register and the comic mismatch with the mundane Soviet setting; “old woman” retains the insulting age/era gap.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Exact match. Preserves the mistaken supernatural interpretation and correctly makes “demons” the agent, which is important for the line’s accusatory comic force.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Maintains the mock-biblical proclamation tone and the religious ‘miracle’ framing that drives the joke in an atheist-modern context.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Perfect match. Captures the emcee/disco-style invitation rather than a flat literal “Everyone dance!”, preserving the comedic anachronism and performative command.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Keeps the breezy, self-satisfied criminal cheerfulness and the idiomatic punchiness needed for the character’s charm.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Exact match. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the Soviet-food-shortage satire and preventing cultural confusion while keeping the comic reveal cadence.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved translation. Preserves the escalating inventory rhythm, the status-item specificity, and the comedic self-incrimination/boasting through counting and trailing ellipses.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Perfect match. Keeps the calm, sing-song fatalism and shared-absurdity punchline about psychiatric ‘treatment,’ which is central to the scene’s darkly comic tone.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
